title: Fancy Ethiopian Penne Pasta Dabo
description: A creamy, earthy, ovo-vegetarian-friendly pasta dish inspired by Ethiopian cuisine. The kombu-cold-extract adds a rich, velvety texture.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the penne pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a separate saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat and saut6 the onion, garlic, and ginger until the onion is translucent.
Add the red lentils and vegetable broth to the saucepan and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the kombu to the saucepan and let it steep for 5 minutes, then remove the kombu and blend the sauce until smooth.
In a small saucepan, melt 2 tbsp of butter over medium heat and add the spices, paprika, and black pepper. Let it simmer for 1 minute, then add the heavy cream and stir to combine.
Add the cooked penne pasta to the cream sauce and toss until the pasta is well coated.
Stir in the niter kibbeh to give the pasta a rich, creamy texture.
Serve the Fancy Ethiopian Penne Pasta Dabo hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers and reheat as needed.
